# instanode.dev

> Zero-setup infrastructure. One HTTP call = real database.

## Machine-readable schema

Full OpenAPI 3.1 spec (every endpoint, request, response, schema):
```
https://api.instanode.dev/openapi.json
```
Import this URL into any OpenAPI-aware tool, MCP server, or agent framework.

## Quick start

Provision a Postgres database (no account, no API key — `name` is required):
```
curl -s -X POST https://instanode.dev/db/new \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"name":"my-side-project"}' | jq
```

Provision a webhook receiver:
```
curl -s -X POST https://instanode.dev/webhook/new \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"name":"github-webhook"}' | jq
```

## Endpoints

### POST /db/new
Provision a Postgres database with pgvector. Body: `{"name":"<label>"}` — required.
Returns `connection_url` (standard postgres:// URL), `token`, `name`, `tier`, `limits`.
Anonymous tier: 10MB storage, 2 connections, expires in 24h.

### POST /webhook/new
Provision a webhook receiver. Body: `{"name":"<label>"}` — required.
Returns `receive_url`, `token`, `name`, `tier`, `limits`, `expires_at`.
POST any payload to `receive_url` to store it. Anonymous: 100 stored, expires 24h.

### POST /webhook/receive/{token}
Send a webhook payload. Accepts any Content-Type.
Returns `{ "ok": true, "id": "<request-id>" }`.

### GET /healthz
Returns `{ "ok": true, "service": "instant-lite" }`.

### POST /api/me/claim
Attach an existing token to your account. Works with a session cookie OR
`Authorization: Bearer <JWT>` so CLIs / agents can claim too. Body:
`{"token":"<uuid>"}`. Paid users get tier='paid' + no expiry automatically.
Idempotent — re-claiming your own token returns the same 200 payload.

### GET /api/me/token
Mint a bearer token for CLI / agent usage. Paid users paste this into
`Authorization: Bearer <token>` on /db/new and /webhook/new to skip the
anonymous 5/day per-subnet cap and get permanent resources without going
through the browser claim flow. 30-day TTL; rotate by calling again.

## Auth for CLIs and agents

Paid-tier callers who don't want to send a browser cookie can authenticate
any endpoint by passing `Authorization: Bearer <JWT>`:

```
TOKEN=$(curl -s -b cookies.txt https://api.instanode.dev/api/me/token | jq -r .token)

curl -s -X POST https://api.instanode.dev/db/new \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"name":"my-agent-db"}'
```

Free-tier (anonymous) callers just omit the header — the 5/day cap applies.

## Response shape (all provisioning endpoints)

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "id": "uuid",
  "token": "uuid",
  "connection_url": "postgres://...",
  "tier": "anonymous",
  "limits": { "storage_mb": 10, "connections": 2, "expires_in": "24h" },
  "note": "Works now. Keep it forever (free 14-day trial): https://..."
}
```

## Tiers

| Tier | Price | Postgres | Webhooks | Expiry |
|------|-------|----------|----------|--------|
| Free | $0 | 10MB / 2 conn | 100 stored | 24h |
| Developer (monthly) | $12/mo | 500MB / 5 conn | 1000 stored | Never |
| Developer (annual)  | $120/yr | 500MB / 5 conn | 1000 stored | Never |

Annual plan is the monthly plan billed once a year at a 17% discount
(two months free). Both unlock the same feature set.

Checkout `plan_id` values: `developer` (monthly), `developer-annual` (yearly).

## No authentication required

All provisioning endpoints work without any headers or API keys.
Anonymous resources expire in 24h. The response `note` field contains a URL
to claim resources permanently with a free account.

## Standard protocols

Connection URL uses the standard Postgres scheme:
`postgres://user:pass@host:port/dbname`

Works with every standard driver. No wrapper SDK needed.
